gaps: The bug scenario involves a specific state: ONE earbud worn + ONE earbud in the OPEN (unplugged) charging case. No existing test covers this asymmetric earbud state where only one earbud is charging while the other is in active use.

The bug involves repeated/duplicate charging notifications (a spamming behavior). No existing test validates notification frequency or deduplication logic — all existing tests only check if a notification appears or not, not whether it appears repeatedly.

The bug uses the Voyager Free 20 (TWS earbuds with separate charge case). Existing tests reference 'headset plugged in' or 'device plugged into charger' — these are generic wired charging scenarios, not the specific TWS case-charging behavior where an earbud is placed into an open case.

No test covers the lifecycle dimension of what happens when the earbud is repeatedly inserted/removed from the open case while the other earbud remains worn. This is a state transition gap (earbud in ear vs. in case, asymmetric).
No test validates charging notification behavior specifically when the charge case is OPEN (unplugged) vs. closed/plugged. The open case state may trigger different charging detection logic.

No test covers the interaction between the worn earbud's active connection state and the charging earbud's notification generation — the app may be incorrectly generating notifications based on the worn earbud's state rather than the charging earbud's state.

generated_testcases.title: [Voyager Free 20] Asymmetric State — One Earbud Worn, Other in Open Case — No Repeated Charging Notifications
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 connected to Poly Studio Mobile app. Both earbuds fully charged and idle.
Wear the LEFT earbud (in ear). Leave the RIGHT earbud in the open (unplugged) charging case.
Observe notification behavior for 60 seconds.
Remove RIGHT earbud from open case, then reinsert it into the open case. Repeat 3 times rapidly.
Observe notification behavior during and after each insertion.
Navigate to: App → Device → Audio Settings. Verify no disruption or notification spam during navigation.
Keep LEFT earbud worn. Close the charging case lid with RIGHT earbud inside. Wait 30 seconds.
Open the charging case lid again (RIGHT earbud still inside). Observe notification behavior.
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: A single charging notification appears when RIGHT earbud is first placed in the open case. No repeated/duplicate notifications appear during the 60-second idle period. No notification spam during repeated insert/remove cycles — at most one notification per insertion event. App navigation remains smooth with no notification interruptions. Closing the case lid does NOT generate a new notification (earbud already charging). Opening the lid does NOT regenerate the notification. Actual (If Bug): Charging notification appears repeatedly every few seconds while one earbud is in the open case and the other is worn. Rapid insert/remove cycles trigger cascading duplicate notifications. Notification spam disrupts app UI.

generated_testcases.title: [Voyager Free 20] Asymmetric State — Notification Deduplication During Extended Wear + Charging
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 connected. LEFT earbud worn, RIGHT earbud in open charging case.
Note the timestamp of the first charging notification.
Wait 2 minutes without touching either earbud.
Count all charging-related notifications received during the 2-minute window.
After 2 minutes, remove RIGHT earbud from case and reinsert. Note new notification timestamp.
Wait another 1 minute. Count notifications again.
Swap: place RIGHT earbud in ear, put LEFT earbud in open charging case. Wait 1 minute.
Count notifications during the swapped asymmetric state.
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Exactly 1 charging notification in the first 2-minute window (the initial one). Exactly 1 new notification after reinsertion (not multiple). Exactly 1 notification after swapping earbuds (for the newly charging LEFT earbud). Total notifications ≤ 3 for the entire test duration. Actual (If Bug): Notifications appear repeatedly every 10-30 seconds during each asymmetric state window, producing 6+ notifications in the first 2-minute window alone. Swapping earbuds does not stop the original notification spam and may add new spam for the other earbud.

generated_testcases.title: [Voyager Free 20] Asymmetric State — App Lifecycle: Background/Foreground During One Earbud Charging
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 connected. LEFT earbud worn, RIGHT earbud in open charging case.
Verify initial charging notification appears (single).
Press Home button to background the Poly Studio Mobile app. Wait 30 seconds.
Reopen the app from recent apps. Observe notification behavior immediately and for 30 seconds after.
Force-kill the app (swipe from recent apps). Wait 10 seconds.
Relaunch the app. Observe notification behavior on reconnect and for 30 seconds after.
While app is in foreground, disconnect Bluetooth from phone settings. Wait 10 seconds.
Reconnect Bluetooth. Observe notification behavior on reconnection.
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: After backgrounding and reopening — no new/repeat charging notification appears (the original single notification state is preserved). After force-kill and relaunch — at most one charging notification appears on reconnection (the app re-syncs state, but deduplicates). After Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect — at most one notification on reconnect. No notification spam in any lifecycle transition. Actual (If Bug): Each foreground transition, app relaunch, or Bluetooth reconnect triggers a fresh burst of repeated charging notifications, as if the asymmetric state is being re-detected and re-notified each time without deduplication.

generated_testcases.title: [Voyager Free 20] Asymmetric State — Open Case vs. Closed Case Charging Notification Behavior
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 connected. Both earbuds in ears, not charging.
Remove LEFT earbud, place it in the open (unplugged) charging case. Keep RIGHT earbud worn.
Observe notification behavior for 30 seconds.
Close the charging case lid (LEFT earbud inside). Observe notifications for 30 seconds.
Open the case lid again. Observe notifications for 30 seconds.
Remove LEFT earbud from case, wear it. Remove RIGHT earbud, place in open case. Repeat steps 2-5 for RIGHT earbud.
Now with one earbud in open case: plug the charging case into USB-C power. Observe notifications.
Unplug the charging case. Observe notifications.
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Step 2 — Single charging notification for LEFT earbud. Step 3 (lid closed) — No new notification (earbud continues charging, state unchanged). Step 4 (lid opened) — No new notification (charging state unchanged). Step 7 (case plugged in) — At most one notification indicating case is charging (not a repeat of the earbud charging notification). Step 8 (case unplugged) — At most one notification. Total notifications ≤ 4 for the entire test. Actual (If Bug): Opening/closing the case lid retriggers the charging notification. Plugging the case into power generates duplicate earbud charging notifications on top of the case charging notification. Notifications cascade and repeat.

generated_testcases.title: [Voyager Free 20] Asymmetric State — Rapid Earbud Swap Between Ear and Open Case (Stress Test)
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 connected. LEFT earbud worn, RIGHT earbud in open charging case.

Perform the following sequence rapidly (each action within 1 second): Remove RIGHT from case → wear RIGHT → remove LEFT from ear → place LEFT in open case → remove LEFT from case → wear LEFT → remove RIGHT from ear → place RIGHT in open case.
Repeat the full swap cycle 5 times consecutively.
After the 5 cycles, stop and let the state settle (LEFT worn, RIGHT in open case).
Count total notifications received from start of step 1 through 30 seconds after step 3.
Open the Poly Studio Mobile app. Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Transparency Mode. Verify all sliders and values are responsive and correct.
Verify the worn earbud (LEFT) still plays audio correctly (play a test track).
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Each earbud insertion into the open case generates at most 1 notification. With 5 swap cycles × 2 earbud insertions per cycle = 10 insertion events, total notifications ≤ 10 (ideally ≤ 10, with no duplicates per event). App remains fully responsive during and after stress test. Transparency Mode sliders respond immediately with no lag. Audio playback on worn earbud is uninterrupted. Actual (If Bug): Each swap cycle generates 3-5 duplicate notifications per insertion event, producing 30+ notifications in seconds. App UI becomes sluggish or unresponsive due to notification flood. Audio stutters or drops on the worn earbud during rapid state changes.

generated_testcases.title: [Voyager Free 20] Asymmetric State — Notification Source Validation: Worn Earbud vs. Charging Earbud
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 connected. Both earbuds worn. Media playing.
Place LEFT earbud in open charging case. Keep RIGHT earbud worn and playing media.
Observe notification content: does it identify the LEFT earbud as charging?
Verify media continues on RIGHT earbud without interruption.
Pause media. Initiate a phone call on the phone. Verify call audio routes to the worn RIGHT earbud.
During the call, remove LEFT earbud from case and reinsert it. Observe notification behavior during active call.
End the call. Check notification history/log in the app (if available) or Android notification shade.
Verify the app's device status screen shows correct state: LEFT charging, RIGHT connected/in use.
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Notification explicitly identifies the LEFT earbud as charging (not ambiguous 'device charging'). Media continues uninterrupted on RIGHT earbud. Call audio routes correctly to RIGHT earbud only. During active call, reinserting LEFT earbud generates at most one notification and does NOT interrupt the call audio. Notification history shows only LEFT earbud charging events. App device status correctly shows asymmetric state (LEFT: charging, RIGHT: connected). Actual (If Bug): Notification is generic ('Earbud charging') without identifying which earbud. Notification incorrectly references the worn RIGHT earbud's state. Call audio is interrupted or rerouted when LEFT earbud is reinserted during the call. App device status shows both earbuds as charging or both as connected.
